title: AMC Entertainment Q4 Results and Box Office Recovery
text:
AMC Entertainment Holdings reported fourth-quarter 2025 earnings that beat analyst expectations, signaling improvement in the cinema industry.

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE
The company generated $1.29 billion in total revenue, surpassing the consensus estimate of $1.27 billion, though this represented a 1.4% year-over-year decline. The net loss of $127.4 million, or $0.18 per share, was narrower than the projected $0.21 per share loss.

Adjusted EBITDA fell to $134.1 million from $164.8 million in the prior-year quarter. Operating cash flow reached $126.7 million with $43.3 million in free cash flow. Year-end cash reserves totaled $428.5 million.

FULL-YEAR RESULTS
For 2025, AMC achieved $4.85 billion in revenue, up from $4.64 billion in 2024. The widened annual net loss of $632.4 million stemmed largely from refinancing charges. However, adjusted EBITDA improved to $387.5 million, and management highlighted "record per-patron revenue" across admissions and concessions.

OUTLOOK
CEO Adam Aron stated that "AMC is exceptionally well positioned to capitalize on a recovering box office," emphasizing the need for quality film releases. The company expressed optimism regarding upcoming major releases and noted January North American box office activity was approximately 16% higher year-over-year.

Stock price declined 1.3% to approximately $1.20 following the announcement.
